final_text:
We can use the relation for diffusion where the diffusion length is proportional to \(\sqrt{Dt}\).  
For the same concentration at the same depth:

\[
\sqrt{D_1 t_1} = \sqrt{D_2 t_2}
\]
\[
D_1 t_1 = D_2 t_2
\]

Given:  
\(D_1 = 5 \times 10^{-14} \, \text{m}^2/\text{s}\) at 400°C  
\(D_2 = 6 \times 10^{-13} \, \text{m}^2/\text{s}\) at 500°C  
\(t_1 = 100 \, \text{hours}\)

\[
t_2 = \frac{D_1}{D_2} t_1 = \frac{5 \times 10^{-14}}{6 \times 10^{-13}} \times 100
\]
\[
t_2 = \frac{5}{60} \times 100 = \frac{500}{60} \approx 8.33 \, \text{hours}
\]

Thus, the correct option is (B).

[ANSWER]B[/ANSWER]
